reasoning: Extensive searches for coups in Africa and Latin America in 2024 and 2025 reveal no clear successful coups before the July 1, 2025 deadline. In Africa, year-end roundups for 2025 identify exactly two successful coups (Madagascar in October and Guinea-Bissau in November) and one failed attempt (Chad in January). Since the successful African coups occurred after July 1, 2025, they do not trigger a positive resolution. We also found failed attempts in DRC (May 2024) and Benin (Dec 2024). In Latin America, the only major military action was the failed attempt in Bolivia (June 2024). Haiti’s government transition in early 2024 was driven by gang violence, which does not meet the criteria of a coup d'état by state apparatus. Finally, while Nicolás Maduro refused to relinquish power after the July 2024 Venezuelan election, this is overwhelmingly characterized as electoral fraud in mainstream media rather than a formal self-coup, and he arguably fails the requirement of having "come to power through legal means" given his disputed 2018 election. Thus, the condition of a successful coup before July 1, 2025 remains unmet.
